verdict_reasoning: BAX (BCL2-associated X) is a pro-apoptotic BCL2 family member. In healthy cells, the majority of BAX resides as a soluble monomer in the cytosol, with a minor pool loosely associated with the outer mitochondrial membrane (OMM). Upon apoptotic stimulation, BAX translocates to and inserts into the OMM — not the plasma membrane — where it oligomerizes to form pores causing MOMP. Its action is entirely at the mitochondrial outer membrane or in the cytosol, never at the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. No CD designation, no TM domain facing the extracellular space, no documented plasma membrane surface pool. Canonical intracellular/cytosolic localization with apoptosis-induced OMM insertion; the protein body is not accessible from outside the cell. pMHC is the only conceivable surface story and is explicitly excluded by triage rules.
